title: cornus alba var. interior
intro: Cornus alba var. interior, also known as Red-stemmed Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to Russia and eastern Asia, characterized by its bright red stems and showy white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cornus alba var. interior include reducing blood sugar levels, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for herbal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal remedy.
